topic: How to Measure AI Coding ROI: Beyond Vanity Metrics

key_points: DX AI Measurement Framework uses human-equivalent hours (HEH) to quantify time savings
Recommends 3-6 months baseline before AI rollout to prevent attribution errors
Tracks adoption weekly, delivery monthly, ROI quarterly
Booking.com used this framework for 3,500 developers, achieving 65% higher adoption and 150K additional hours saved
Net ROI Formula = [(Productivity Gain – Quality Cost) / Tool Cost] × 100
Highest-impact use cases: debugging, refactoring, test generation, documentation — NOT new code generation
Tracks 7 metrics: Code Survival Rate, PR Revert Rate, Defect Density, Rework Rate, Cyclomatic Complexity, Review Iterations
Teams that get the most value support onboarding, track results regularly, and connect tool use to broader engineering goals
Individual developer productivity gains often don't translate to organizational delivery improvements
DORA metrics inflate under AI assistance — more deployments but hidden quality issues
Real ROI question: 'Did we deliver more customer value?' not 'Did we ship more code?'
DX Core 4 framework: speed, effectiveness, quality, business impact
Segment engineering metrics by AI involvement — tag AI-generated vs human-written code separately
Compare quality, review time, and defect rates against human-only baselines
Review overhead for AI-generated code is often higher than human-written code
AI-generated code introduces subtle bugs that pass CI but fail in production
GitHub Copilot: 20 million users by July 2025, 4.7 million paid subscribers by January 2026
55% faster task completion in controlled studies
Takes 11 weeks for developers to fully realize productivity gains
Most developers judge Copilot in first week experiencing only 20% of its potential

stats.fact: Developers feel 24% faster with AI coding tools but METR study showed they were actually 19% slower — then still reported 20% perceived improvement afterward
Vendors claim 50-100% productivity gains, but measured reality shows only 5-15% organizational improvements
Bain's 2025 Technology Report: AI coding tools deliver only 10-15% productivity gains despite adoption by two-thirds of software firms
Healthy ROI on AI coding tools is 2.5-3.5x average, 4-6x for top-quartile teams — but only when cost denominator includes actual token and usage-based costs
Agentic AI tools (e.g., Claude Code) can cost $200-$2,000+ per engineer per month in token spend
Average AI-assisted code share is 15-25% of lines written; top-quartile teams at 40-60%
GitHub Copilot: 88% of generated code stays in final version; industry average acceptance rate is 44%
CodeRabbit December 2025 report: ~1.7x more issues found in AI-coauthored PRs vs. human-only PRs
Organizations that undercount costs and skip quality measurement routinely overstate ROI by 20-40%
Every 1-point increase in Developer Experience Index saves 13 minutes per developer per week (~10 hours annually)
Average hours saved per developer per week: 3-5 hours (average), 5-8 hours (top quartile) — must be discounted by 60% utilization factor
Industry average: 8-55% throughput lift for AI-assisted vs. human-only PRs; AI code shows 20-55% faster PR cycles
A major financial services firm tracked 30% increase in PR throughput YoY for AI users vs. 5% for non-AI users
90% of Fortune 100 companies use GitHub Copilot; 50,000+ organizations total

angles: The perception gap: Why developers feel more productive but teams ship the same (or less)
Hidden costs of AI coding tools: token spend, review overhead, rework, onboarding time
Why DORA metrics lie about AI productivity — and what to track instead
The 'quality tax': AI-generated code has 1.7x more issues, silently eroding your ROI
A step-by-step framework to establish baselines and measure true AI coding ROI
Real enterprise case studies: what Booking.com and financial services firms actually measured
Time-to-value problem: why 11 weeks is needed before ROI materializes
Segment your metrics: AI-touched vs. human-written code as separate measurement streams
keywords: how to measure AI developer productivity
outline: H2: Why Most AI Coding ROI Measurements Are Wrong
H2: The Vanity Metrics Trap (and What They Hide)
H2: The True Cost of AI Coding Tools
H2: Step 1 — Establish Baselines Before You Buy
H2: Step 2 — Segment AI-Touched vs. Human-Written Code
H2: Step 3 — Track Quality, Not Just Speed
H2: Step 4 — Calculate Real ROI with the Net ROI Formula
H2: Step 5 — Measure Business Impact, Not Just Engineering Metrics
H2: Real-World Benchmarks to Compare Against
H2: Tooling That Helps You Measure Accurately
